topic: A person finds out their article was published under someone else's name
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara stared at the screen, her breath catching in her throat. The headline, the perfectly crafted prose, the very essence of the piece she had poured her soul into—it was all attributed to a stranger. A slow, incredulous smile spread across her face, starting at the corners of her eyes and pulling her cheeks upward. Her fingers hovered over the keyboard, trembling slightly, before she let them drop onto the desk with a soft clack. "No," she whispered, the sound barely audible over the hum of the computer. She reread the byline, the name that wasn't hers, a name she vaguely recognized from a forgotten archive. A sudden, buoyant energy surged through her, making her want to leap to the window. This wasn't a mistake; this was an unexpected, dazzling detour. She started typing immediately, a wild, excited energy propelling her forward.

[story 2]

Mark leaned back in his chair, a slow, wide grin spreading across his face as he absorbed the email. His eyes scanned the text, then landed on the author credit. His jaw dropped, and he let out a soft, surprised chuckle that quickly escalated into a full, unrestrained laugh. He slapped his knee, the sound echoing slightly in the quiet office. "Well, I'll be," he murmured, shaking his head as if to clear the sudden rush of exhilaration. He felt a warmth bloom in his chest, a giddy lightness that made his limbs feel buoyant. He picked up his phone, his thumbs already flying across the screen, already formulating plans. This was more than just a find; it was a spectacular, unexpected gift.

[story 3]

When the notification popped up, Anya physically recoiled for a split second, then let out a sharp, involuntary gasp. Her hands gripped the edge of her desk until her knuckles turned white. She blinked rapidly, trying to process the impossible sight: her meticulously researched essay, complete with her unique voice, published under an entirely different moniker. A bright flush crept up her neck and across her cheeks. A bubbling sensation started low in her stomach, spreading outward, making her want to bounce on the balls of her feet. She let out a short, sharp laugh—a sound of pure, unadulterated delight—and immediately began scrolling through the publication's website, a restless, eager energy vibrating through her entire body.
